completed: Added a lean runtime retrieval schema for Quran and hadith passages with pgvector search support

commands: npx tsx -e "import { loadHadithPassages, loadQuranPassages } from './server/src/corpus/retrieval-passages.ts'; const run = async () => { const quran = await loadQuranPassages('./server/.tmp/quran_en.json', {}); const hadith = await loadHadithPassages('./server/.tmp/hadith', {}); console.log(JSON.stringify({ quran: quran.length, hadith: hadith.length, sampleQuran: quran[0]?.reference, sampleHadith: hadith[0]?.reference }, null, 2)); }; void run();"
validation.notes: Root and server typecheck passed. Normalization was dry-run against live source files downloaded into a temporary local folder; the parser produced 6,236 Quran passages and 14,735 hadith passages across the default Bukhari and Muslim whitelist.

risks: The selected hadith-json dataset does not expose per-hadith grade metadata, so this pipeline cannot enforce Sahih/Hasan filtering inside a collection without swapping datasets
The SQL migration is currently written for vector(768); if EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS changes, the migration must be updated to match before seeding
follow_ups: Select and prepare the production source files under server/corpus/source
Author manual overrides for context summaries and emotional tags

Implement request-time retrieval and citation assembly in the intervention backend
